habituation_segments.description: Global segmentation designating consumer's position in habituation journey based on recency and frequency, with regional nuances

power_users.definition: Highest frequency and most recent shoppers. Top tier of engagement.
power_users.characteristics: Contribute majority of GPV. Highest lifetime value. Most engaged with app.
power_users.typical_behavior: Shop multiple times per month. High AOV. Low churn risk.
power_users.marketing_strategy: Retention and loyalty programs. VIP treatment. Exclusive offers.

habituated.definition: Regular shoppers with consistent purchase patterns. Established habit.
habituated.characteristics: Moderate to high frequency. Predictable purchase cycles.
habituated.typical_behavior: Shop every few weeks. Reliable revenue stream.
habituated.marketing_strategy: Maintain engagement. Cross-sell new products. Prevent churn.

occasional.definition: Infrequent shoppers. Lower engagement but not churned.
occasional.characteristics: Purchase a few times per year. Lower frequency than habituated.
occasional.typical_behavior: Shop for specific occasions or needs. Price sensitive.
occasional.marketing_strategy: Increase frequency. Promote new use cases. Seasonal campaigns.

occasional_at_risk.definition: Occasional shoppers showing signs of decreased engagement.
occasional_at_risk.characteristics: Declining purchase frequency. Extended time since last order.
occasional_at_risk.typical_behavior: May be exploring competitors. Needs re-engagement.
occasional_at_risk.marketing_strategy: Win-back campaigns. Incentives to return. Address pain points.

power_users_at_risk.definition: Previously high-value customers showing churn signals.
power_users_at_risk.characteristics: Historically high frequency but recent decline. High risk of churn.
power_users_at_risk.typical_behavior: Extended gap since last purchase. May have found alternatives.
power_users_at_risk.marketing_strategy: Urgent retention efforts. Personalized outreach. Premium incentives.

power_users_lapsed.definition: Former power users who have stopped shopping.
power_users_lapsed.characteristics: Long period of inactivity. Previously high value.
power_users_lapsed.typical_behavior: No recent orders. Likely using competitors.
power_users_lapsed.marketing_strategy: Aggressive win-back. Understand churn reasons. Competitive offers.

churned.definition: Consumers who have stopped using Afterpay for extended period.
churned.characteristics: No activity for 12+ months. Low probability of return.
churned.typical_behavior: Inactive account. May have switched to competitors.
churned.marketing_strategy: Reactivation campaigns. Understand barriers. New value propositions.

onboarder.definition: New consumers in first few months. Still establishing habits.
onboarder.characteristics: Recently signed up. May have 0-2 orders.
onboarder.typical_behavior: Exploring product. Learning how to use. High potential.
onboarder.marketing_strategy: Onboarding education. First purchase incentives. Build habit.

never_active.definition: Signed up but never completed first order within 12 months.
never_active.characteristics: Registered account with no purchase history.
never_active.typical_behavior: Abandoned onboarding. May have concerns or barriers.
never_active.marketing_strategy: Activation campaigns. Remove barriers. First order incentives.

oncers.definition: Consumers who made exactly one purchase and never returned.
oncers.characteristics: Single order only. Did not establish habit.
oncers.typical_behavior: One-time use. May have had poor experience.
oncers.marketing_strategy: Second purchase campaigns. Address experience issues. Build loyalty.

regional_variations.note: Segment definitions vary by region (AU, NZ, US, UK, CA) based on local consumer behavior patterns
regional_variations.example: Power user thresholds differ between ANZ (higher frequency) and US markets

lifecycle_stages.description: Consumer journey stages from acquisition through retention

stages.credit_contract_accepted: Accepted terms. Ready to shop. Event: production.event.paylater.consumer.account.credit-contract-accepted.v1
stages.first_order: Completed first purchase. Becomes NAC (New Active Customer)
stages.established: 3+ successful money movements in 30 days. ESTABLISHMENT_DATE tracked
stages.repeat_customer: Multiple orders. Building habit and loyalty
stages.loyal_customer: Consistent purchases over extended period. High lifetime value

generation_segments.description: Age-based consumer segmentation. Calculated from birth_year obtained during IDV
generation_segments.note: ~25% of consumers haven't completed IDV so generation data is incomplete

gen_z.characteristics: Digital natives. Mobile-first. Value transparency and authenticity.
gen_z.afterpay_relevance: Core target demographic. High BNPL adoption. Prefer flexible payments over credit cards.
gen_z.shopping_behavior: Fashion, beauty, lifestyle. Influenced by social media. Frequent small purchases.

gen_alpha.characteristics: Youngest generation. Limited purchasing power currently.
gen_alpha.afterpay_relevance: Future opportunity. May use through Families/Teen accounts.
gen_alpha.shopping_behavior: Emerging. Influenced by parents and online content.

millennial.afterpay_relevance: Significant user base. Higher AOV than Gen Z. Established shopping habits.
millennial.shopping_behavior: Home goods, electronics, travel. Value convenience and flexibility.

gen_x.characteristics: Independent. Skeptical of marketing. Value quality.
gen_x.afterpay_relevance: Growing segment. Higher income. Larger purchases.
gen_x.shopping_behavior: Practical purchases. Home improvement. Quality over quantity.

baby_boomer.characteristics: Traditional values. Less tech-native. Higher disposable income.
baby_boomer.afterpay_relevance: Smaller segment. Requires education on BNPL benefits.
baby_boomer.shopping_behavior: Established brands. Higher ticket items. Less frequent purchases.

data_fields.birth_year: Year of birth from IDV. Used to calculate age and generation
data_fields.age: Calculated from birth_year. Current age of consumer
data_fields.generation: Derived field categorizing consumer by birth year cohort

gender_segments.note: Gender data obtained during IDV or inferred from consumer behavior
gender_segments.data_usage: Used for demographic analysis and targeted marketing campaigns

country.usage: Primary geographic segmentation. Determines currency, regulations, product availability

state_province.usage: State-level analysis. Identifies regional opportunities and penetration

metro_flag.usage: Urban vs rural segmentation. Metro areas typically have higher penetration

suburb_city.usage: Granular geographic analysis. Identifies specific neighborhoods and communities

postcode.usage: Zip/postal code level analysis. Used for UK region mapping and DMA analysis

dma.description: Designated Market Area for US. Used for advertising and regional analysis

income_segments.description: Household income segmentation based on zip code data
income_segments.note: Inferred from geographic data, not directly collected
income_segments.usage: Aligns with banking product strategy. Core customers often in 30k-70k range

fashion.characteristics: High volume. Frequent purchases. Strong Gen Z appeal.

beauty.characteristics: Loyal customer base. High repeat rate. Premium pricing.

retail.description: General merchandise, department stores, specialty retail
retail.characteristics: Broad product range. Varied AOV. Omnichannel presence.

home_garden.characteristics: Higher AOV. Less frequent purchases. Project-based.

electronics.characteristics: High AOV. Considered purchases. Tech-savvy audience.

sports_outdoors.description: Athletic wear, sporting goods, outdoor equipment

jewelry_accessories.characteristics: Premium pricing. Gift purchases. Special occasions.

pets_vets.description: Pet supplies, veterinary services, pet care

merchant_size_segments.description: Segmentation based on merchant GPV and scale

enterprise.definition: Largest merchants. Multi-million dollar annual GPV

mid_market.definition: Medium-sized merchants. Strong regional or category presence

smb.definition: Small to medium businesses. Independent retailers
smb.characteristics: Local or online-only. Single or few locations. Owner-operated.

emerging.definition: New or small merchants just starting with Afterpay
emerging.characteristics: Testing BNPL. Building customer base. Limited volume.

online.characteristics: Higher conversion. Broader reach. Digital-first merchants.

instore.description: Physical retail locations using Afterpay card or barcode
instore.characteristics: Immediate gratification. Brick-and-mortar presence. Strong in AU.
instore.note: Requires app download and card setup by consumer

omnichannel.description: Merchants accepting both online and instore
omnichannel.characteristics: Highest value consumers. Cross-channel shopping behavior.
omnichannel.note: Omnichannel consumers have higher lifetime value

products.pi4_pay_monthly: Both Pi4 and Pay Monthly options
products.gift_cards: Merchant gift cards available in Afterpay app
products.sup_enabled: Accessible via Single Use Payment (major retailers)
products.ads_affiliates: Participating in Afterpay advertising and shop directory

merchant_lifecycle.description: Stages in merchant relationship with Afterpay

stages.prospect: Not yet live. In sales pipeline.
stages.newly_launched: Recently went live. First 90 days. Honeymoon period may apply.
stages.established: Consistent volume. Stable performance. Beyond 90 days.

marketing_audience_naming.description: Standardized naming conventions for marketing campaigns and audience lists

values.newsl: Newsletter campaigns for updates and curated content
values.lifecl: Lifecycle campaigns based on customer stage

by_lifecycle: churn-50d (at risk, 50 days since last order)

consumer_demographics.description: Source of truth for consumer master data

merchant_demographics.description: Source of truth for merchant master data

vertical_mapping.description: Vertical data assigned to each merchant

order_demographics.description: Order-level data with consumer and merchant demographics

consumer_demographics: Global Merchant QBR Template (2024) - includes generation, gender, location demographics
Afterpay Orders - Habituation Segment field available
Order date cohort - Consumer's segment at beginning of month

consumer_behavior: Power users contribute majority of GPV despite being smaller segment
Gen Z is core demographic but growing across all age groups
Metro areas have higher penetration than non-metro
Omnichannel consumers have significantly higher lifetime value
Habituation segments vary by region due to cultural shopping differences
merchant_patterns: Fashion and beauty are largest verticals by volume
Online channel dominates but instore strong in Australia
Enterprise merchants drive bulk of GPV but SMB important for diversity
Honeymoon periods help new merchants test Afterpay with lower fees
Vertical performance varies significantly by country
regional_differences: ANZ: Higher instore adoption, more established market, higher frequency
US: Largest market, online-focused, growing Pay Monthly
UK: Strong Clearpay brand, online-dominant, fashion-heavy
CA: Smaller market, similar patterns to US
